title: Weekly Market Report | Jun 6-12, 2026
summary:
# 📊 Weekly Market Report | June 6–12, 2026

---

## 🇯🇵 Japan Equity Markets — Weekly Overview

Japan's equity market entered a full correction phase this week following the historic year-to-date high of 68,786 (Nikkei 225, June 3). However, the decline was concentrated in AI/semiconductor leaders — a healthy rotation rather than a broad selloff.

**Nikkei 225 Weekly Trajectory:**
- Mon (6/8): Opened ~65,900, volatile on SQ (derivatives expiry)
- Tue-Wed (6/9-10): Range-bound 64,800–65,200 zone
- Thu (6/11): Closed 64,217 — apparent short-term floor
- Fri (6/12): Rebounded to ~65,400 on Iran/geopolitical relief

**Weekly change: -2,371 pts (-3.6%)** | Trading value remained elevated at Y12–15T/day.

### Key Observations

- Over 70% of TSE Prime stocks ROSE this week — a selective correction, not a broad breakdown
- Major SQ (Special Quotation) expiry week amplified intraday volatility
- Foreign investor profit-taking in AI/semicon; domestic retail buying defense and value names

---

## 🇺🇸 US Markets & Global Events

### 🚀 SpaceX IPO (SPCX) — History Made Today

- **IPO Price: $135/share** | **Raised: ~$75B** | **Valuation: ~$1.77T**
- Demand 4x oversubscribed ($250B+ orders vs $75B offered)
- 30% retail allocation — unprecedented for a mega-IPO
- Comparable in scale to Tesla, Apple at their respective peaks

### 🔧 Marvell Technology (MRVL) +25%+ Weekly

- Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang called Marvell 'the next trillion-dollar company' at Computex (Taiwan)
- Nvidia-Marvell AI custom chip (ASIC) partnership: revenue to exceed $10B by FY2029
- Positive read-through for Japan's electronic component makers (Murata, TDK, Taiyo Yuden)

### 🌐 Iran Geopolitical Risk: Easing

- Trump cancelled planned strikes on Iran; peace deal signaled in principle
- WTI crude dropped sharply
- Thursday US session: Dow +1.86% (+923 pts), S&P 500 +1.75%, Nasdaq +2.54%

---

## 📊 Theme Performance Rankings (Week)

### ▲ Top 5 Rising Themes

### #1: MLCC / Electronic Components +10.2%
Direct beneficiary of the Marvell/Nvidia AI partnership announcement. Japan's leading component suppliers (Murata, TDK, Taiyo Yuden) saw concentrated buying as Computex-driven AI capex enthusiasm spread through the supply chain.

### #2: Defense & Aerospace +6.5%
Sustained by Iran tensions (even as easing), Trump's NATO defense spending demands, and PM Kishida's '17 Strategic Domains' policy that explicitly includes defense. Mitsubishi Heavy, Kawasaki HI, IHI led gains.

### #3: Resources (LNG/Hydrogen) +4.1%
While oil fell on Iran relief, LNG and natgas-linked names held firm on stable demand. European LNG export expansion and energy security themes provided a floor. INPEX led.

### #4: Buffett Picks (Trading Companies) +3.2%
Risk-on rotation into Berkshire-held Japanese trading companies (Mitsubishi Corp, Mitsui & Co, etc.). Yen weakness tailwind and stable commodity prices support earnings outlook.

### #5: EV / Battery +2.8%
Reports of Chinese EV majors entering Japan market and EU EV subsidy continuations drove interest. Panasonic, Prime Earth EV Energy among gainers.

---

### ▼ Bottom 5 Falling Themes

### #1: Rare Earth / Resources -7.9%
China signaled potential new rare earth export controls, reigniting supply chain fears for semiconductors and EVs. Sumitomo Metal Mining and Toho Titanium among hardest hit.

### #2: Generative AI / AI Datacenter -6.8%
Profit-taking after driving 5+ weeks of sharp gains. Fund rebalancing and quarter-end position adjustments added pressure. SoftBank Group, Nvidia-linked names at the center of selling.

### #3: Semiconductor Equipment -5.4%
Tokyo Electron, Advantest, Lasertec gave back gains after last week's surge. Renewed China export restriction reports added to downward pressure.

### #4: Quantum Computing -4.9%
Speculative buying from prior weeks unwound; skepticism about near-term commercialization timelines resurfaced.

### #5: Crypto / Web3 -4.2%
Capital rotated toward SpaceX IPO. Continued regulatory headwinds in major jurisdictions added pressure.

---

## 🔮 Next Week Watch List (Jun 15–19)

### SPCX — History's Largest IPO: First Week Trading
How SpaceX prices at open and performs in its first week will create ripple effects: index inclusion demand, Nasdaq capital flows, and sentiment for Japan's space/AI-related names.

### FOMC Meeting (Jun 17-18)
Rate hold widely expected (5.25-5.50%). Key: Powell's language on inflation trajectory and rate cut timing. Any hint of 2026 cuts = yen weakness and Japan equity tailwind. Hawkish surprise = risk-off.

### Iran Peace Talks
Resolution = crude stays low = risk-on continuation. Breakdown = oil spike, defense bid, risk-off. Weekend headlines will set the tone for Monday open.

### Japan Economic Data
- Jun 16 (Mon): Bank of Japan May meeting minutes
- Jun 18 (Wed): May Trade Statistics — export strength test
- Jun 19 (Thu): National CPI (May) — most critical data point for BoJ rate path

---

## 💡 StockWaveJP Editorial View

This week's correction is a classic 'pause' after the historic 68,786 high — not a trend reversal.

**Scenarios for Next Week:**

Bull Case (55% probability): SPCX strong open + Iran deal confirmed + FOMC neutral = Nikkei recovery to 66,000-68,000. MLCC/defense/trading company rotation sustains; AI names bottom and rebound.

Neutral Case (30% probability): SPCX disappoints OR FOMC hawkish OR Iran re-escalates — Nikkei tests 64,000. 63,000-64,000 range acts as strong support; correction extends 1-2 more weeks.

Bear Case (15% probability): Multiple headwinds coincide — Nikkei tests 61,000-range. Medium-term trend intact; would represent a buy-the-dip opportunity rather than a trend change.
